findings.title: Drift (negative) test omits a subset of no-write boolean flags
findings.location: packages/control-plane/test/service.test.js — "Test And Golden Case Set Secondary workflow path rejects drift in no-write metadata"
findings.description: assertControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetSecondaryWorkflowPathResult currently enforces every boundary flag to its fixed value, but the drift test only exercises tampering of a subset (runtime/authz/engine/trimming/audit/test/golden flags + blocked_refs/behavior/subphase). It does not assert the validator rejects tampered service_logic_execution_permitted, runtime_route_created, database_write_permitted, storage_write_permitted, product_state_persistence_permitted, no_real_data, or writes_product_state. Their correct values are asserted on the generated result ('emits metadata-only receipt'), but no negative test guards the validator's enforcement of them, so a future regression that dropped one of those validator checks would not be caught.
findings.recommendation: Add these flags to the drift override table (each flipped to its unsafe value) so the negative test pins the validator's fail-closed enforcement for all no-write flags, matching the strictness already present in the validator and RP00 live checks.
findings.boundary_impact: None at runtime — the validator enforces all flags today and the slice writes nothing; this is a defense-in-depth test-coverage gap only, with no effect on production_ready boundary behavior.
fix_refs: packages/control-plane/test/service.test.js#Test And Golden Case Set Secondary workflow path rejects drift in no-write metadata
findings.fix_evidence: Added negative drift assertions for service_logic_execution_permitted, runtime_route_created, database_write_permitted, storage_write_permitted, product_state_persistence_permitted, no_real_data, and writes_product_state; focused drift test and full service test pass after the fix.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-036 adds a metadata-only RP00.P02.M07.S08 Test And Golden Case Set Secondary workflow path that consumes only the S07 Primary happy path result and emits a frozen, no-write secondary receipt handing off to RP00.P02.M07.S09. The slice is fully and consistently mirrored across contract definition + current_subphase + subphase_closeouts row + production_ready_requires, policies.js (result fields + policy), service.js (prechecker module + getter + precheck fn), validators.js (assert policy + assert result), the synthetic fixture (exact precheck_result mirror), 6 service tests, the RP00 validator (weighted boundary terminal=false→S09, definition/policy/prechecker/getter/assert checks, live positive + 11 negative checks, README/test text gates, construction-inspection gate), and the README handoff row. The prechecker validates the full S07 shape, requires entrypoint=test_and_golden_case_set, marker RP00.P02.M07.S07, carried allow_metadata_only/applied=true, a present happy_path_receipt and non-blank audit_event_ref, and fails closed on all 30 forbidden claims plus any unknown truthy claim. All boundary flags (test/golden/audit/permission/authz/engine/trimming/service/route/db/storage/product-state) are false and validator-enforced; matter_id remains null; no real data or secrets are used. Evidence files (manifest/command-evidence/claude-review/adjudication/construction-inspection) are correctly in the intentional pending-review/production_ready:false state that this single valid pack-level review resolves. node --check passes on all 5 JS files and all 6 JSON files parse. One P3 test-coverage nit; no boundary or correctness defects.

boundary_checks.consumes_only_s07_primary_happy_path: pass - requires entrypoint_id=test_and_golden_case_set, primary_happy_path_subphase_id=RP00.P02.M07.S07, primary_happy_path_decision=allow_metadata_only, primary_happy_path_applied=true, present happy_path_receipt, non-blank audit_event_ref, and full assertControlPlaneTestAndGoldenCaseSetPrimaryHappyPathResult validation
boundary_checks.hands_off_to_s09: pass - next_subphase=RP00.P02.M07.S09 in policy/prechecker/service/validator/fixture/contract; weighted boundary nextSubphaseId=RP00.P02.M07.S09, terminal=false
boundary_checks.no_test_execution: pass - test_execution_permitted=false (validator-enforced), doesNotExecuteTests=true; no test runner invoked by the slice
boundary_checks.no_golden_fixture_generation_or_persiste: pass - golden_fixture_generation_permitted=false, golden_fixture_persistence_permitted=false; doesNot* flags set and enforced
boundary_checks.no_golden_catalog_or_unit_test_contract_: pass - catalog/contract refs are only carried forward as strings; mutate_golden_case_catalog and mutate_unit_test_contract are forbidden claims; doesNotMutate* flags set
boundary_checks.no_audit_write: pass - audit_ledger_append_permitted=false, audit_event_write_permitted=false; append_audit_ledger/write_audit_event/mutate_audit_ledger are forbidden claims
boundary_checks.no_runtime_permission_authz_engine_trimm: pass - runtime_permission_evaluation_permitted/authz_evaluator_call_permitted/permission_engine_execution_permitted/security_trimming_execution_permitted all false and validator-enforced; corresponding claims forbidden
boundary_checks.no_db_storage_product_state_write: pass - database_write_permitted/storage_write_permitted/product_state_persistence_permitted=false, writes_product_state=false; result frozen, pure object construction only
boundary_checks.fail_closed_on_forbidden_and_unknown_cla: pass - any truthy forbidden claim rejected by name; any truthy unknown claim rejected as unknown; non-object claims rejected; verified by tests and RP00 live negative checks
boundary_checks.no_real_data_or_secrets: pass - synthetic tenant.synthetic.lawfirm / user.synthetic.control-plane-admin only; no_real_data=true; use_real_*/use_credentials_or_secrets forbidden
boundary_checks.matter_id_boundary: pass - validator requires matter_id===null until a Matter-scoped input exists; receipt.matter_id mirrors null
boundary_checks.does_not_complete_m07_p02_rp00: pass - completesSourceMicroPhase=false; scope_policy does_not_complete_RP00_P02_M07/RP00_P02/close_RP00=true; does_not_replace_H00_or_C00=true; weighted terminal=false
boundary_checks.hrx_boundary_untouched: pass - changes confined to control-plane package, contract, RP00 script, and CP00-036 evidence; no HRX files touched
boundary_checks.evidence_pending_by_design: pass - manifest in_progress/production_ready:false, claude-review pending, adjudication pending, construction-inspection PENDING_CLAUDE_REVIEW_AND_FINAL_GATES; this review is the single valid pack-level review the scaffold awaits
boundary_checks.cross_file_consistency: pass - contract definition, policy, prechecker, validator expectations, fixture precheck_result, tests, README, and RP00 validator are mutually consistent (deepEqual/JSON.stringify-enforced); required_input_fields equals S07 result fields
boundary_checks.review_is_read_only: pass - only diff/read/parse and node --check (parse-only) were used; no repository state mutated

parsed_review.residual_risk: Low. The slice is metadata-only, deterministic, frozen, and fail-closed; all destructive/runtime capabilities are disabled and validator-enforced, and the change is mechanically mirrored across contract/policy/service/validator/fixture/test/README/RP00-script with array-equality gates. The pack correctly remains not production_ready pending the final gates (this single valid pack-level review, finding adjudication, construction inspection, RP00/full validation, and commit); the manifest's noted pre-evidence RP00 failure is the expected scaffold-order condition. Residual risk is confined to the one P3 negative-test coverage nit, which carries no current boundary impact. No tests were executed, no fixtures/catalog/contract/audit were generated or mutated, and no repository state was changed during this review.

I have completed a thorough read-only review of CP00-036. I verified the full chain — contract definition, policy, prechecker, service function, validators, fixture mirror, README handoff, RP00 validator coverage, tests, and evidence files — for internal consistency, boundary preservation, and fail-closed behavior, and confirmed the S08 implementation faithfully mirrors the established S07 pattern. All JS files parse, all JSON parses, and the only "extra receipt fields" observation is the documented codebase convention (also present in S07), so it is not a defect.

Here is my review:

adjudication_notes: Claude produced pass_with_findings with no P0/P1/P2. CP00-036-F1 was P3 only and was fixed by adding missing no-write negative drift assertions, then rerunning the focused drift test and full service test successfully.
